$comment: PROPOSED data schema for the 1:1 attack/combat port. Generated by the Fable 5 attack-mechanics audit (2026-07-03). This is a SCHEMA, not data: every field carries an evidence/confidence tag telling cheaper agents whether real values exist yet and where they come from. NO field in this file contains a fabricated gameplay value. Cross-reference: research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md and research/decomp/data/attack-mechanics-ledger.json.

inputBindings.$comment: The ROM has a per-actor COMMAND STRUCT (actor+0x4b0 -> pointer), written by player-input and CPU-AI code, read by the shared actor dispatch. The port should mirror this: PlayerInput -> resolved AttackCommand, so player and AI share one command vocabulary.

buttonsHeld.evidence: command struct bytes +0/+1 copied to actor+0x660/+0x661 (chunk_0007.c:21-42, FUN_800562b8)

knownValues.5: ranged variant B (extra targeting setup, chunk_0007.c:4756)

command.evidence: commandStruct+4 -> actor+0x591 (chunk_0007.c:23; chunk_0009.c:980); consumed by FUN_8005d494 attack-active handler
command.confidence: DERIVED_ROM (value routing); BLOCKED (which button/context produces which value — see ledger contextual_attack_resolution)

hyperActivate.evidence: FUN_80069814 (chunk_0009.c:104-115): transformed button field actor+0x5d4 bit 0x200 sets actor+0x6fb = 6

contextualAttacks.$comment: Wiki taxonomy lists 8 melee contexts (standing/ground-dash/air-dash/flying/stabbing/jumping/neutral-air/landing). NO asset-side context encoding exists (animation banks are flat (group,slot) enums) and the ROM command byte only distinguishes 2 melee + 3 ranged variants so far. Do not hard-code 8 contexts as final until the +0x591 writer decision tree is decoded.

movementContexts.$comment: Port-side enum for capturing the actor's movement situation at attack press. The values are the wiki taxonomy; the ROM equivalent is the +0x6fe state-slot (35-entry table 0x802d3570) which the resolver decision tree presumably reads. Capture-only until the decision tree is decoded.
movementContexts.confidence: WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY as a full set; ROM state slots exist (DERIVED_ROM, behavior-notes.md (u)) but slot->context labels are Likely, not Confirmed

hitboxReferences.$comment: Real per-borg hitbox data already has a decoded container format.

attackHitboxes.source: pl####hit.bin 0xAA0-0x1E9f: 64 x 0x50 records with activeStart/activeEnd anim-frame windows (vs actor+0x1cdc); selected via remap B slot by spawner kind (zz_008ac80_ @0x8008ac80)

recordFields.damageRecordIndex: u16 +0x04 -> 0x18-stride damage table bound at actor+0x27c

attackHitboxes.openMapping: attack-animation (group,slot) -> remap-B slot -> record indices is NOT decoded; BLOCKED

projectileProperties.$comment: ROM projectiles are generic objects (spawn helper zz_008893c_ @0x80088938), type byte +0x11, hit-record table pointer +0x2c, per-object rehit counter +0x4e refilled from damage-record s8+0x16.

solidity.confidence: WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY — no projectile-vs-projectile logic found in the generic collision passes; if it exists it is per-object-type dispatch (PTR_FUN_802da740). DO NOT implement as final behavior.

penetration.$comment: Proposed enum, mapped to what the ROM actually shows. The ROM has no single 'penetration' flag; destruction-after-hit is actor/object-type gated, and re-hit pacing comes from rehitIntervalFrames. Collision gating observed: hit allowed if (attacker record flagsB & 0x4000)==0 OR (target record flagsB & 0x2000)!=0 (chunk_0003.c:7080).

enum.persist_with_rehit_interval: DERIVED_ROM mechanism (object+0x4e counter refilled from record+0x16)
enum.collision_flag_gated: DERIVED_ROM partial (flagsB 0x4000/0x2000 gate at chunk_0003.c:7080; semantics not fully proven)

penetration.confidence: DERIVED_ROM (mechanisms), BLOCKED (per-move values: all 9 extracted borg-family records have rehitIntervalFrames = 0; the generic/comhit table DAT_802c4760 is extracted but per-move binding is not)

refillMode.$comment: DERIVED_ROM end-to-end mechanism; per-borg VALUES come from the same stat row that already feeds HP (borg-hp-stat-rows-802f2988.json).

weaponCells.stride: 0x8 bytes from +0x774 (cur ammo s16, prev ammo s16, refill timer f32)
weaponCells.auxCells: from +0x78c stride 0x8 (max ammo s16 @+0x78e, refill type s16 @+0x790, refill param s16 @+0x792)
weaponCells.confidence: DERIVED_ROM (chunk_0007.c:60-94 init; chunk_0009.c zz_006dbe0_ fire gate @0x8006dbe0; zz_006dcc0_ per-frame refill @0x8006dcc0; zz_006de10_ one-shot grant @0x8006de10)

refillTypes.0_or_negative: all-at-once: timer counts down, on expiry cur=max
refillTypes.1: gradual: timer-driven fractional grant toward max
refillTypes.2_3: special modes (init observed chunk_0007.c:73-94; runtime semantics unread)
refillTypes.deployedReturn: deployed-object handlers restore ammo when the object dies/fails to spawn (chunk_0029.c:2270-2301, chunk_0036.c)

refillMode.hyperInteraction: refill rate multiplied by FLOAT_804376c8 when actor+0x6fc set (zz_006dcc0_)
refillMode.confidence: DERIVED_ROM; exact per-borg values already extracted for weapon 0 in research/decomp/data/borg-hp-stat-rows-802f2988.json; weapons 1/2 rows need re-extraction

statusEffects.$comment: Framework fields proven in ROM; per-status semantics mostly open.

immunityMask.type: u32 (victim+0x5a0, bit N = immune to status N; also zeroes HP damage)

paramTier.type: s8 tier (+0x74a) + s8 accum (+0x74e) + s16 timer 900 (+0x750); tier indexes PTR_DAT_804339e0 param rows -> actor+0xb8/+0x1dd0/+0x5f8

statusEffects.openQuestions: per-status-id semantics (which id = poison/freeze/etc.), DoT loop (NOT found), scale LUT DAT_802dd57c indexing, +0x768 field role conflict (see open-questions doc)

hyperModifiers.$comment: actor+0x6fb (byte countdown, set to 6 by Y-gate FUN_80069814) arms activation; zz_005b2b8_ @0x8005b2b8 consumes it and sets actor+0x6fc=1 (+0x6fa=1). +0x6fc effects proven: outgoing damage x2.0, incoming damage x0.5, ammo refill rate x FLOAT_804376c8.

meter.type: UNKNOWN — no accumulator found in the damage path

speedModifiers.confidence: CHECKED_CLOSED for +0x6fc-gated speed; wiki speed claim remains WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY (may run through param tier or another field)

resistanceFalloff.$comment: The wiki's hidden per-target damage resistance IS the combo-rank falloff already ported: dmg *= DAT_802c7ca0[victim+0x6ca] unless record flagsB & 0x4000 (zz_003cd5c_ step 16). DO NOT add a second resistance layer.

friendlyFire.$comment: Same-team damage x0.25 unless record flagsA & 0x1000; (flagsA & 0x1000) + victim+0x59c & 0x1000 instead applies the /40 block divisor. Ported in computeSourceDamage. The wiki's 'OHKO ignores friendly fire' has NO ROM counterpart found (no OHKO damage path exists; damage floors at 1).

blockDivisor.confidence: DERIVED_ROM (FLOAT_80437028), ported but caller never sets blockDivisorActive
